These verses are the first verses that the prophet sallallah sallallah.aii wasallam received and were the verses with which he was sent as a messenger to the people of Quraysh. In these verses, Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala gives very particular instructions on not only the prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam and sending him as a messenger to his people, but specific instructions with how to organize his da'wah or his invitation or his calling of his people to Islam. There are things which are haram and there are things which are more haram. There are things which are obligatory and there are those things which are more obligatory. There are issues which are of shik and there are issues which are of minor shik. There are issues of major kufur and minor kufur major vul minor vul major minor neph etc. All of these things are the details which are necessary in order to have a fruitful daer. It's not that all of the commandments were you know just sent down upon the prophet sallall.aii wasallam all at once. It wasn't just one book that was sent down to him all at once. It was organized. It came in parts and it began with the most important then that which is important then that which is of of secondary importance etc. And so this is why the scholars of Islam have put so much energy and have concentrated their efforts so much on detailing the order in which the chapters and verses of the Quran were revealed and also the sun and narrations of the prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam as much as possible because of this order. This order it doesn't just give us the order of the prophet's story or a timeline. It's not just a historical thing. It's an issue of it reflects what is most important, what is of lesser importance, what is most dire, what is not as dire, etc. If we understand this, we look not only at the order of the chapters and how they were revealed, but also the order of the verses. So we notice first of all that the first word or the first five verses that Allah sent to the prophet sallall.aii wasallam, they began with the word which is to read or to recite. The next group or cluster of verses that were revealed to him began with the word the first command. Right? So first we have recite and read. Then we have stand up and go forth and give da. What do we understand from this? What do we learn from this? We learn from this what imar understood is one of the core principles of Islam and that is as alkari says in his That knowledge comes before action and speech. Any action that is not predicated on knowledge is going to be misguided. And any knowledge that is not succeeded by action, it is what incurs the anger of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. And generally, holistically, they fall into one of these three categories. Number one, those whom Allah's grace favor is upon. Number two, those whom Allah's anger is upon. Number three, those who are misguided. Who are those whom Allah's grace is upon? They are those who know. They are upon knowledge and they implement that knowledge. They are those who know and act upon that knowledge. Who are those whom Allah's anger is upon? Those who have knowledge but fail to act on it. Who are those who have gone astray? They are those who act upon ignorance. they do not have knowledge. So we have the the only type of person that is successful are those who have action with knowledge. They know and they implement. summary: This lecture discusses the first verses revealed to Prophet Muhammad (Quran 96:1-5, Surah Al-Alaq) and their foundational importance in Islam. The speaker explains how these initial revelations emphasize the principle that knowledge must precede action, as evidenced by the command to 'read' before the command to 'stand and give da'wah.' The speaker elaborates on three categories of people mentioned in Surah Al-Fatihah: those whom Allah's favor is upon (those with knowledge and action), those upon whom Allah's anger is upon (those with knowledge but no action), and those who are astray (those with action but no knowledge). The lecture emphasizes that true guidance requires both knowledge and implementation together, warning against the dangers of good intentions without knowledge, and the spiritual emptiness of knowledge without action.
suggested_title: Knowledge and Action: The Foundation of Islam - Understanding Surah Al-Alaq and Surah Al-Fatihah
catchy_title: Why Knowledge Must Come Before Action
